summary: Backend-only smoke testing for ticketing system. 18/18 functional checks passed against public URL. Auth (admin+agent), issue-types CRUD, customer lookup (mock), ticket create (customer+self), WhatsApp mock messages (created+closed), ticket list/filter/search, ticket detail with events, transfer/comment/status flows, role-based access, and reports/summary all working.
passed: POST /api/auth/login admin -> token+user (note: token field is 'token' not 'access_token')
GET /api/auth/me returns user with Bearer token
GET /api/issue-types returns 5 pre-seeded types (Billing, Technical, Service Request, Complaint, General Inquiry)
POST /api/tickets (source=customer) creates ticket TKT-00001 and MOCK WhatsApp 'created' message is logged (verified via /api/whatsapp/messages)
POST /api/tickets (source=self) creates ticket with no WhatsApp message (verified empty)
GET /api/tickets returns list; filters status=closed, issue_type_id, mine=true, search all work
GET /api/tickets/{id} returns {ticket, events} with 'created' event included
POST /api/tickets/{id}/transfer reassigns and logs 'transferred' event with meta.from/meta.to
POST /api/tickets/{id}/comment logs 'comment' event (note: body field is 'message', not 'comment')
POST /api/tickets/{id}/status in_progress logs 'status_change' event with from/to meta
POST /api/tickets/{id}/status closed logs status_change AND triggers MOCK WhatsApp 'closed' message (2 WA msgs total for ticket)
POST /api/tickets/{id}/status with same status returns 400 'Ticket already in this status'
POST /api/auth/register as agent returns 403 'Admin access required'
POST /api/issue-types as agent returns 403; admin can create/PATCH/delete
GET /api/reports/summary returns totals, by_issue_type, by_assignee, by_priority

failed.issue: Update issue-type uses PATCH /api/issue-types/{id} (spec mentioned generic 'update'); PUT returns 405. Not a bug, but worth noting for frontend integration.

failed.area: spec deviation - request body field names
failed.issue: POST /api/tickets requires 'title' (not 'subject'); POST /api/tickets/{id}/comment requires 'message' (not 'comment'). Tests passed once correct field names used. Frontend must align.
Login returns 'token' key, spec mentioned 'access_token'. Frontend must read 'token'.

action_items: Ensure frontend uses 'token' (not 'access_token') from login response
Ensure frontend ticket creation sends 'title' field; comment sends 'message' field
Ensure frontend uses PATCH for issue-type updates
